soil_types: Well-Drained Soil With High Organic Matter

pests.name: Diamondback Moth
pests.impact: Larvae are small, light green caterpillars. Immature larvae damage the underside of leaves, while mature larvae eat leaves in a circular pattern. High populations can consume entire leaves, leaving only veins.
pests.solution: Regular nursery inspection and maintenance, constant field inspection, crop rotation, destruction of crop residues after harvest, field sanitation, avoid unnecessary insecticide application to protect natural enemies. If pest population reaches damaging levels, apply Emamectin benzoate 5%SG (8g/16l), Etofenprox 100g/l EC (24ml/16l), Chlorfluazuron 50g/l EC (16ml/16l), Bistrifluron 100g/l EC (24ml/16l), Tebufenozide 200g/l SC (24ml/16l), Chromafenozide 50g/l SC (32ml/16l), Chlorantraniliprole 200g/ SC (6ml/16l), Lufenuron 50g/l EC (16ml/16l), Spinosad 25g/l SC (16ml/16l), or Flubendiamide 24% WG (6ml/16l).

pests.name: Looper Caterpillar
pests.impact: Green caterpillar that moves in a semi-loop. Eats large holes in leaves. Caterpillar waste can be seen at the base of leaves. High populations can consume entire leaves, leaving only veins.
pests.solution: Regular nursery inspection and maintenance, constant field inspection, crop rotation, destruction of crop residues after harvest, field sanitation, avoid unnecessary insecticide application to protect natural enemies. If pest population reaches damaging levels, apply Emamectin benzoate 5%SG (8g/16l), Etofenprox 100g/l EC (24ml/16l), Chlorfluazuron 50g/l EC (16ml/16l), Bistrifluron 100g/l EC (24ml/16l), Tebufenozide 200g/l SC (24ml/16l), Chromafenozide 50g/l SC (32ml/16l), Chlorantraniliprole 200g/ SC (6ml/16l), Lufenuron 50g/l EC (16ml/16l), Spinosad 25g/l SC (16ml/16l), or Flubendiamide 24% WG (6ml/16l).

pests.name: Tobacco Cutworm
pests.impact: Larvae feed in clusters, consuming the entire plant and leaving only veins.
pests.solution: Regular nursery inspection and maintenance, constant field inspection, crop rotation, destruction of crop residues after harvest, field sanitation, avoid unnecessary insecticide application to protect natural enemies. If pest population reaches damaging levels, apply Emamectin benzoate 5%SG (8g/16l), Etofenprox 100g/l EC (24ml/16l), Chlorfluazuron 50g/l EC (16ml/16l), Bistrifluron 100g/l EC (24ml/16l), Tebufenozide 200g/l SC (24ml/16l), Chromafenozide 50g/l SC (32ml/16l), Chlorantraniliprole 200g/ SC (6ml/16l), Lufenuron 50g/l EC (16ml/16l), Spinosad 25g/l SC (16ml/16l), or Flubendiamide 24% WG (6ml/16l).

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.impact: Larvae are most active at night, cutting young plant stems at the base. Mature larvae can be seen eating leaves. Damage is more prevalent in dry weather conditions.
pests.solution: Deep plowing exposes larvae and pupae to the soil surface, making them vulnerable to predatory birds. Maintain good weed control in and around the field, and practice field sanitation. Regularly inspect the field and manually collect hidden larvae when the population is low. If damage is severe, apply recommended insecticides such as Profenophos 500g/LEC (32ml/16l) or Etofenprox 100g/LEC (24ml/16l) immediately after planting.

pests.name: Root Maggot
pests.impact: Larvae feed on developing roots and young stems. Infested young plants show wilting and twisted leaves. White maggots can be found when examining the root system of such plants.
pests.solution: Constant field inspection. Use yellow sticky traps (greased yellow polythene sheets placed near the ground) to control adult flies. Practice crop rotation. Uproot and burn infested plants along with the larvae. Deep plowing to destroy pupae. Use properly prepared organic fertilizers. If damage is severe, apply recommended insecticides such as Fipronil 50 SC.

pests.name: Aphid
pests.impact: Population increases rapidly under dry weather conditions. Winged and wingless adults can be seen on the underside of leaves. These 1-3 mm soft-bodied insects come in various colors like green, yellow, and purple. They suck sap from plants, causing leaves to curl and reducing yield quality.
pests.solution: Monitor the crop from the beginning, for example, by placing yellow water-filled basins coated with adhesive in various places in the field. Maintain good weed control in the field to remove host plants. Protect natural enemies of aphids, which are abundant in the environment, by avoiding unnecessary insecticide applications. If the aphid population is high, apply Thiamethoxam 25% WG (5g/16l), Imidacloprid 70%WG (2g/16l), Thiocyclam (Hydrogen Oxalate) 50% SP (40g/16l), Chlorantraniliprole 20% + Thiamethoxam 20% WG (5g/16l), Buprofezin 25% SC (45ml/16l), or Azadirachtine 5%EC (16ml/16l).

pests.name: Snails And Slugs
pests.impact: Damage is commonly observed during periods of high rainfall. They cause irregular feeding damage to leaves, which hinders crop growth and reduces market value.
pests.solution: Improve field sanitation. Maintain good weed control. Remove potential hiding places in the field during the day. Inspect the field at night and manually collect and destroy them. Use beer traps as bait in various spots in the field. Apply recommended molluscicides such as Metaldehyde 3% RB (10-40kg/ha), Metaldehyde 4% RB (10-40kg/ha), or Metaldehyde 5% RB (10-40kg/ha).

diseases.name: Cabbage Clubroot
diseases.impact: The disease is more likely to occur when the soil is acidic.
diseases.solution: Use clean seeds. Increase soil pH by adding lime. Practice crop rotation with non-Brassica crops. Remove other host plants from the field. Use clean tools.

diseases.name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
diseases.impact: Not explicitly stated, but implies spots on leaves.
diseases.solution: Keep the field clean. Use recommended fungicides such as Maneb, Mancozeb, Propineb, or Tebuconazole.

diseases.name: Black Rot
diseases.impact: Not explicitly stated, but implies decay.
diseases.solution: Use clean, disease-free seeds for planting. Maintain good drainage in the field. Keep the field clean. Practice crop rotation with non-Brassica crops. Regularly inspect the field and remove diseased plants. Use recommended fungicides such as Chlorothalonil.

diseases.name: Head Rot
diseases.impact: Not explicitly stated, but implies decay of the cabbage head.
diseases.solution: Practice field sanitation. Use recommended fungicides.

diseases.name: Cabbage Leaf Spot
diseases.impact: Not explicitly stated, but implies spots on leaves.
diseases.solution: Use clean, disease-free seeds for planting. Keep the field clean. Remove other host plants from the field. Maintain proper spacing between plants. Use recommended fungicides such as Hexaconazole or Teboconazole.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.solution: Keep the field clean. Control weeds. Use recommended fungicides such as Captan, Maneb, or Mancozeb.
